goal: Produce a full-year scope and sequence for Algebra I aligned to CCSS-M. The output is a unit-by-unit outline covering the entire school year, with each unit including: unit title, primary CCSS-M standards addressed, approximate duration in instructional days, essential questions, and a single end-of-unit test as the summative assessment. No lesson-level planning. No differentiation flags.

inputs: CCSS-M Algebra I domain standards (A-SSE, A-APR, A-CED, A-REI, F-IF, F-BF, F-LE, S-ID)
NYC DOE standard school year: approximately 180 instructional days
School context: Columbia Secondary School, NYC DOE, grades 6–12
NY State Next Generation Algebra I standards: NOT yet available — do not apply; flag for a future revision pass
approach: Organize units into a coherent progression: foundations → linear → systems → functions → quadratics → statistics
Size units so the full year sums to approximately 160–170 instructional days (leaving buffer for review, assessments, and school events)
Assign CCSS-M standards to units; each standard should appear in exactly one primary unit (may appear as review in another)
Each unit ends with one end-of-unit test (no other summative assessments)
do_not_include: Section-level variation (ALG-01, ALG-02, ALG-03 share the same scope)
NY State Next Generation standards (pending spec from teacher)

constraints.ny_regents: Algebra I Regents is the external summative anchor; scope should prepare students for it
